name: Config Validate Implementation

tasks.title: Implement config validate command
tasks.body:
Create cmd/config/validate.go with a newValidateCmd(app) function following the existing command pattern (see show.go, set.go for reference). The command should:

1. Load the three-tier config via app's ConfigRepository.Load()
2. Run config.ValidateStructure(cfg) to check structural constraints
3. Optionally run config.Validate(cfg) for full validation (with a --full flag)
4. Collect errors and warnings into a structured report
5. Human output: print each issue with severity, category, and description. Print a summary line (e.g., '2 errors, 1 warning'). Use output.PrintHuman for normal output, output.PrintError for errors
6. JSON output: emit a structured envelope via output.Print(output.Ok(...)) containing the issues list, error count, and warning count
7. Exit code: return nil (exit 0) when no errors found, return an error (non-zero) when errors exist. Warnings alone should not cause non-zero exit
8. Register the command in cmd/config/register.go's Register() function

Reference the existing command pattern in cmd/config/show.go and the validation functions in internal/config/validate.go.
acceptance_criteria: Command is registered and appears in 'wolfcastle config --help'
Loading a valid config and running 'wolfcastle config validate' exits 0 with clean output
Loading a config with structural errors exits non-zero and reports each error
The --json flag produces structured JSON output with issues array and counts

tasks.title: Write tests for config validate command
tasks.description: Write tests for config validate command
tasks.body:
Create cmd/config/validate_test.go with comprehensive tests following the test pattern in cmd/config/show_test.go. Use testutil.NewEnvironment to set up a temporary .wolfcastle directory. Tests should cover:

1. Valid config: command exits 0, human output shows 'no issues' or similar clean message, JSON output has empty issues array and zero counts
2. Config with structural errors (e.g., empty pipeline, invalid stage references): command exits non-zero, human output lists each error, JSON output contains the errors
3. Config with warnings but no errors: command exits 0 (warnings are non-fatal)
4. The --full flag runs full validation including identity checks
5. Missing or unreadable config: command returns an appropriate error
6. Both human and JSON output modes for each scenario

Maintain >90% coverage on the new validate.go file.
acceptance_criteria: All tests pass with 'go test ./cmd/config/ -run TestValidate'

tasks.title: Write CLI documentation for config validate
tasks.description: Write CLI documentation for config validate
tasks.body:
Create docs/humans/cli/config-validate.md documenting the new command. Follow the format and style of existing CLI docs (see docs/humans/cli/config-show.md for reference). Include:

1. Command synopsis and description
2. Flag documentation (--full for full validation, --json for structured output)
3. Exit code semantics (0 = clean, non-zero = errors found)
4. Example usage for CI pipelines and pre-commit hooks
5. Description of what ValidateStructure checks vs full Validate
acceptance_criteria: Document exists and covers command usage

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Config Validate Implementation is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify the command is registered in cmd/config/register.go and appears in help output
Verify exit code behavior: 0 for clean, non-zero for errors, 0 for warnings-only
Verify JSON output envelope matches the project's output.Ok() pattern
Verify test coverage exceeds 90% on cmd/config/validate.go
